statement: Member has confirmed cervical radiculopathy.
Nerve root compression is documented.
Radicular pain in a dermatomal distribution is documented.
Numbness in a dermatomal distribution is documented.
Weakness in a dermatomal distribution is documented.
Member has confirmed cervical myelopathy.
Spinal cord compression is documented.
Gait disturbance is documented.
Bilateral upper extremity weakness is documented.
Hyperreflexia is documented.
Positive Hoffman sign is documented.
Bowel or bladder dysfunction is documented.
Member has confirmed cervical disc herniation.
Objective neurological deficit is documented.
Member has an MRI within 6 months of the request.
Member has a CT myelogram within 6 months of the request.
The imaging demonstrates structural pathology consistent with the member's symptoms.
Member has completed at least 6 weeks of physical therapy.
The physical therapy was supervised.
The physical therapy was documented with functional outcomes.
The physical therapy was directed at the cervical condition for which surgery is requested.
Member trialed NSAIDs or COX-2 inhibitors for at least 4 weeks.
Member trialed muscle relaxants for at least 4 weeks.
Member trialed neuropathic agents for at least 4 weeks.
Member trialed oral corticosteroids for at least 4 weeks.
Member received at least one cervical epidural steroid injection.
Member received a cervical medial branch block.
Member received a trigger point injection series.
Member has confirmed cervical myelopathy per criterion 2.1(b).
Myelopathy is the primary diagnosis for the case.
Member has completed at least 4 weeks of physical therapy.
The treating physician has documented that interventional treatment poses a risk of neurological deterioration.
Current imaging within 6 months demonstrates structural pathology consistent with the member's symptoms and the proposed surgical level.
The imaging was interpreted by a radiologist.
The imaging was interpreted by the treating specialist.
The treating physician has provided attestation that conservative treatment has been completed.
The treating physician has provided clinical rationale for surgical intervention.
The treating physician has provided a description of functional limitations.
The treating physician has provided a statement of surgical risks and alternatives considered.
